heading: Annual and sick leave
label: 210–1 Annual and sick leave

D, title III, § 302136 Stat. 2136Sept. 30, 2022“For purposes of annual leave accumulated in fiscal year 2022, the authority provided in section 2106 of division C of ( note) shall apply to such leave by substituting ‘2022’ for ‘2020’ in subsections (a) and (d)(2).”Public Law 116–15942 U.S.C. 210–1, , , provided that:Pub. L. 116–159, div. C, title I, § 2106134 Stat. 729Oct. 1, 2020“(a).—In GeneralNotwithstanding section 219 of the Public Health Service Act (), a commissioned officer of the Public Health Service who, except for this section, would lose at the end of the fiscal year 2020 accumulated annual leave in excess of 60 days, may retain such amounts of accumulated annual leave in excess of 60 days.42 U.S.C. 210–1“(b).—Use of Excess LeaveAnnual leave retained pursuant to subsection (a) shall be lost unless it is used by the officer no later than .September 30, 2023“(c).—ApplicabilityThis section shall not apply to an officer on terminal leave preceding separation, retirement, or release from active duty, as of the effective date specified in subsection (d).“(d).—Effective DateThis section shall become effective on the earlier of—“(1)the date of the enactment of this Act []; orOct. 1, 2020“(2).”September 30, 2020

Availability of Funds, , provided for the availability of funds for payment of compensation for prior accumulated and accrued leave for any officer under section 3 of this Act.Act Aug. 9, 1950, ch. 654, § 464 Stat. 427

Leave Regulations, , provided that:Act Aug. 9, 1950, ch. 654, § 564 Stat. 427“Except insofar as the provisions of this Act [enacting this section, amending , and enacting provisions set out as notes under this section and ] are inconsistent therewith, leave regulations adopted prior to the enactment of this Act [], pursuant to the Public Health Service Act [ et seq.], shall remain in effect until repealed, amended, or superseded.”section 210 of this titlesection 210 of this title42 U.S.C. 201Aug. 9, 1950

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: a Regulations
children.text: In accordance with regulations of the President, commissioned officers of the Regular Corps and officers of the Ready Ready Reserve Corps on active duty may be granted annual leave and sick leave without any deductions from their pay and allowances.1So in original.1

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: b Limitation
children.text: The regulations described in subsection (a) may authorize accumulated annual leave of not more than 120 days for any commissioned officer of the Regular Corps or officer of the Ready Reserve Corps on active duty.

children.heading: Definitions2See 1962 Amendment note for subsec. (d) below.2
children.label: c Definitions2See 1962 Amendment note for subsec. (d) below.2
children.text: For purposes of this section the term “accumulated annual leave” means unused accrued annual leave carried forward from one leave year into a succeeding leave year, and the term “accrued annual leave” means the annual leave accruing to an officer during one leave year.
